name: San Pedro
description: This site off Bermuda features dramatic underwater cliffs and caves teeming with colorful reef fish. The clear Atlantic waters offer excellent visibility, often exceeding 100 feet. Divers explore swim-throughs and overhangs covered in vibrant coral formations. Look for parrotfish, angelfish, and the occasional reef shark. The site's steep walls create exciting drift dive opportunities. Water temperatures stay comfortable year-round, making it accessible for most divers. The combination of geological features and marine life creates a memorable diving experience.

name: Curlew
description: This wreck dive in Bermuda sits upright in clear Atlantic waters at 30 feet, perfect for beginners. The intact steel hull teems with colorful reef fish, while moray eels peek from its holds. Strong currents sometimes sweep the site, bringing pelagics like barracuda. The shallow depth allows long bottom times to explore the coral-encrusted structure. Visibility often exceeds 100 feet, showcasing the wreck against white sand. Night dives reveal octopus and lobsters emerging from the metal corridors.

name: Blanche King
description: This site off Bermuda features a dramatic underwater cliff plunging into the deep Atlantic. Vibrant coral formations cling to the wall, teeming with parrotfish, angelfish, and occasional reef sharks. Crystal-clear visibility often exceeds 100 feet, with mild currents ideal for drift diving. The steep drop-off creates stunning light effects as sunlight filters through the blue. Watch for sea turtles gliding along the wall and schools of jacks patrolling the depths. Warm water temperatures make this a year-round diving destination.
